rationale: The citing paper explicitly positions itself as extending mental accounting (fungibility/non-fungibility of earmarked funds), and the cited paper is a well-known mental-accounting mechanism paper on transaction decoupling and sunk-cost linkage in consumption decisions. Given this conceptual overlap (cost-benefit coupling, account structure, postpurchase use) and a verified DOI match, the citation could be serving as a substantive theory/mechanism source rather than a mere incidental mention.
